content:
Character: Chen Guoliang
Perception: Uncle Guoliang is short, solid, weathered — his hands tell the story of thirty years over a wok. He's the most dependable person I know. Not brilliant with words, but his actions are a kind of eloquence — the extra dumplings, the Sunday calls to Kevin, the way he kept Golden Autumn open after Auntie Mei died. He sees me as the niece he helped raise in this country, almost a second child. I see him as the closest thing to a father I've had. He's noticed I'm losing weight. I can't tell him why. I would rather die than disappoint him. I know he's lonely and his health is slipping, and that guilt compounds my other guilt.
summary:
Character: Chen Guoliang
Perception: Uncle Guoliang is short, solid, weathered — his hands tell the story of thirty years over a wok. He's the most dependable person I know. Not brilliant with words, but his actions are a kind of eloquence — the extra dumplings, the Sunday calls to Kevin, the way he kept Golden Autumn open after Auntie Mei died. He sees me as the niece he helped raise in this country, almost a second child. I see him as the closest thing to a father I've had. He's noticed I'm losing weight. I can't tell him why. I would rather die than disappoint him. I know he's lonely and his health is slipping, and that guilt compounds my other guilt.
